name: Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop
name_original: Castellar Viejo de la Frontera - Parque Natural Los Alcornocales – circular desde San Roque
description: Cycle the moderate 21.7-mile Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop through Los Alcornocales Natural Park, gaining 1310 feet.
region_intro: Touring cycling around San Roque offers diverse landscapes, from coastal stretches along the Mediterranean Sea to inland hilly areas. The region features Mediterranean environments, pine woods, and open spaces, providing varied topography for cyclists. Routes often traverse areas near Los Alcornocales Natural Park, known for its cork oak forests, and parts of the EuroVelo 8 network. The terrain includes moderate elevation gains, offering panoramic views of the surrounding countryside and the Rock of Gibraltar. Best touring cycling routes around San Roque The most popular touring cycling route is Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop, a 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores inland areas, potentially offering views of the nearby natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gibraltar Tunnel – Ibrahim-al-Ibrahim Mosque loop from Playa de La Atunara, a moderate 17.9 miles (28.7 km) path. This route provides coastal views and proximity to Gibraltar. Local touring cyclists also love the Scenic Rural Road – Iron bridge loop from Club de Golf Valderrama, a 19.4 miles (31.2 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Touring cycling around San Roque is defined by coastal paths, hilly areas, and natural parks like Los Alcornocales. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in San Roque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1,100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore San Roque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in San Roque?
A: San Roque offers an extensive network of touring cycling routes, with over 60 routes recorded on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy rides to more challenging expeditions.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in San Roque?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain, from scenic coastal paths offering stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea and the Rock of Gibraltar, to inland hilly areas with moderate elevation gains. Routes often traverse Mediterranean environments, pine woods, and open spaces, including sections near natural parks like Los Alcornocales.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in San Roque?
A: Thanks to its Mediterranean climate, San Roque is suitable for touring cycling year-round. However, the months of May and July are particularly popular among cyclists, offering pleasant weather conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in San Roque?
A: Yes, San Roque offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33928137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar border crossing – View of the Rock of Gibraltar loop from San Roque - La Línea&lt;/a&gt;, which is 26 km long and provides gentle terrain with scenic views.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, San Roque has several difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31590966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Carmen – Entrance to Monte La Alcaidesa loop from Guadiaro&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 50 km and features significant elevation changes, offering a rewarding experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views of the Rock of Gibraltar and pass through or near the cork oak forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park. You can also discover impressive viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/341143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Skywalk&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3748220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Top Station of the Gibraltar Cable Car&lt;/a&gt;, and explore natural wonders such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3748096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, the region around San Roque is rich in history. You can cycle to historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/670899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de la Frontera Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a 13th-century fortress offering panoramic views. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5371075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Duquesa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3748197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moorish Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in San Roque?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in San Roque are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981394312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31577227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Rural Road – Iron bridge loop from Club de Golf Valderrama&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of Gibraltar?
A: Absolutely. Several routes provide stunning views of the Rock of Gibraltar. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38286329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Tunnel – Ibrahim-al-Ibrahim Mosque loop from Playa de La Atunara&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate coastal path that brings you close to Gibraltar, offering excellent photo opportunities.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in San Roque?
A: The touring cycling routes in San Roque are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 out of 5 stars from over 220 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of coastal and inland scenery, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass through natural parks?
A: Yes, some routes offer the chance to cycle through or alongside natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981394312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores inland areas that provide glimpses into the nearby Los Alcornocales Natural Park, famous for its cork oak forests.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in San Roque?
A: One of the longer touring cycling routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31590966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Carmen – Entrance to Monte La Alcaidesa loop from Guadiaro&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 50 km. This route is classified as difficult due to its length and elevation changes, making it suitable for cyclists looking for an extended ride.
tips: A good bike lane between San Roque Station and Almoraima, 7 km long and is part of EuroVelo 8 (Mediterranean route)

Castellar Viejo, also known as "El Castillo" is one of the three population centers that are in the municipality of Castellar de la Frontera. The most representative of this point is the great fortress of Arab origin whose wall surrounds the old town since the 13th century. The castle itself has been converted into a hotel.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It requires good fitness and includes a mix of paved and unpaved sections, with approximately 400 meters of elevation gain. While the komoot estimate for moving time is around 2 hours and 16 minutes, expect the full experience, including stops, to take up to 5 hours, especially if you're exploring the sights.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, typical of Andalusia. You'll encounter a mix of mostly paved roads and challenging unpaved segments, including gravel roads that can range from well-maintained to more demanding climbs. The route features winding sections with both uphill and downhill gradients, offering varied cycling conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the loop?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking landscapes and cultural points of interest. You'll cycle through diverse environments, including sections of EuroVelo 8 Cycle Path: San Roque to Castellar, and pass by the historic Castellar de la Frontera Castle. Don't miss the panoramic views from the Guadarranque Reservoir Viewpoint. The area is also known for its picturesque 'white villages' (Pueblos Blancos) and the natural beauty of Los Alcornocales Natural Park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts near San Roque. You can typically find parking in or around the town of Castellar de la Frontera or San Roque, which are common starting points for routes in the area. Look for public parking areas in these towns.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop incorporates sections of the broader Transandalus network, which is a significant bikepacking route spanning Andalusia. It also utilizes parts of the Andalucía (Province of Cádiz) and Transandalus official trails, among others like Calzada Dehesa Boyal and Paseo Fernando Medina Mena.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop?
faqs.answer: Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are generally the best times. The weather is milder and more pleasant for cycling. Summer can be very challenging due to extreme heat, often requiring riders to adapt by taking midday siestas to avoid the highest temperatures.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Transandalus: Castellar de la Frontera Loop?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas in Andalusia, specific regulations can apply within Los Alcornocales Natural Park. It's best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural park areas, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in Los Alcornocales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for cycling on established routes within Los Alcornocales Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of protected areas. For specific activities or large groups, it's advisable to check with the park's official information points.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, varied terrain, and approximately 400 meters of elevation gain, this route might be challenging for absolute beginners. Cyclists should have good basic fitness and some experience with touring cycling, including handling both paved and unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around Castellar de la Frontera and nearby towns offers opportunities for cultural immersion. You'll find traditional towns and villages where you can access cafes, pubs, and various accommodation options. These spots are perfect for resting and refueling during or after your ride.

faqs.question: What makes this loop unique compared to other routes in the region?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a unique blend of being part of the extensive Transandalus network, providing a taste of this significant bikepacking route. It combines the natural beauty of Los Alcornocales Natural Park with the cultural richness of the 'white villages' and historic sites like Castellar de la Frontera Castle, all while utilizing quiet country roads for a relaxing touring experience.

waypoints.name: EuroVelo 8 Cycle Path: San Roque to Castellar
